# Africa Global Forum

> A peer network for the African diaspora — where Africans abroad help each other, sit together to share what they're going through, and bounce ideas. Africa Global Forum (AGF) insists on creating opportunity among ourselves first, then with the world.

Africa Global Forum (AGF) is led by Samuel Kimani Sikuku and operates at the corridor between Africa and its diaspora. We are a network — not a media brand and not a consultancy — and our work falls into three areas: peer help among diaspora members, community channels for those going through similar things, and live talks with open Q&A. AGF also runs affiliate partnerships with companies operating the Africa ⇄ Abroad corridor (past partners include Lemfi and Founders Running Club), and publishes free research on remittance, migration, capital and entrepreneurship.

## What we do

- **Help & Be Helped** — A diaspora marketplace to give, receive and pay it forward. Search the network for members who can help.
- **You Are Not Alone** — A dedicated channel to share what you're carrying and find others who carry it too. Working name: "We are together."
- **Bounce Ideas** — Inspiring talks from members, with an open Q&A floor afterwards.

## Reports

All Africa Global Forum reports are free to download.

- [African Migrants · Top Destinations — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-migrants-top-destinations-2026-rebuilt.pdf): Where Africans are moving in 2026 — corridors, cities and what they're looking for. 13 pages.
- [Africa's Growing Economies — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/africa-growing-economies-2026-rebuilt.pdf): Where growth is coming from across the continent — sectors, capitals and capital flows. 12 pages.
- [Remittance Apps in Africa — 2026 YTD](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/remittance-apps-africa-2026-ytd.pdf): The latest snapshot of the apps moving money along the corridor. 11 pages.
- [Best Countries for African Students — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/best-countries-african-students-2026-rebuilt.pdf): Where to study, work and stay — ranked for African students looking abroad. 15 pages.
- [African Diaspora Entrepreneurs — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-diaspora-entrepreneurs-2026-rebuilt.pdf): Founders, operators and businesses Africans abroad are building. 10 pages.
- [Return Migration — Africa 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/return-migration-africa-2026-rebuilt.pdf): Who is going home, why, and what the returning diaspora is reshaping. 10 pages.
- [Blockchain & Cross-Border Africa — 2025](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/blockchain-cross-border-africa-2025.pdf): How on-chain rails are reshaping cross-border value across Africa and its diaspora. 8 pages (archive).

## Founder

- Samuel Kimani Sikuku, Founder of Africa Global Forum. Based between Nairobi and London.

## Partners (Africa ⇄ Abroad corridor)

- Lemfi — fintech / remittance
- Founders Running Club — community

## Membership

Application-based. Fellowships available — no member turned away for cost. The application form is being polished; follow updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/98224182/

## Contact

- Website: https://africaglobalforum.com/
